title: Contributing Guidelines
text: repository structured standard R package following conventions outlined Writing R extensions manual. additional files provided part built R package listed .Rbuildignore, .travis.yml, used continuous testing integration.

title: Contributing Guidelines
text: code package found R/, (except compiled source code, used, /src). functions thoroughly documented roxygen2 notation; see Documentation.

title: Contributing Guidelines
text: new feature bug-fix include unit-test demonstrating change. Unit tests follow testthat framework files tests/testthat. Please make sure testing suite passes issuing pull request. can done running check() devtools package, also check consistent documentation, etc. package uses travis continuous testing mechanism R ensure test suite run push Github. icon top README.md indicates whether tests currently passing.

title: Contributing Guidelines
text: function documentation generated automatically. Please edit documentation files man/ NAMESPACE. Instead, construct appropriate roxygen2 documentation function files R/ . documentation generated running document() function devtools package. Please consult Advanced R programming guide workflow unfamiliar . Note functions include examples documentation. Please use \dontrun examples take seconds execute require internet connection. Likewise, README.md file base directory edited directly. file created automatically code runs examples shown, helping ensure functioning advertised consistent package README vignette. Instead, edit README.Rmd source file compile RStudio knit button (ctrl+shift+k) rmarkdown::render() .

title: Contributing Guidelines
text: manuscript files found vignettes directory. Text hard-wrapped less 80 characters width possible. allows git better track real changes files improves display line-based diffs. reason, also avoid re-wrapping text frequently, changing line end encodings, etc. Embedding images: Image generation handled markdown file, embed online png images published imgur .md output, vector pdf graphics .pdf manuscript. Citations: Citations added .Rmd file using pandoc markdown notation, corresponding bibtex entries added components/references.bib. Citations can also added standard markdown link.

title: Meta Data for Met Data
text: metamet R package attempts solve many problems encountered working meteorological observation data. provide system : standardising metadata converting file formats converting variable naming conventions converting units automating QA/QC facilitating manual QA/QC via shiny app imputing missing values “gap-filling” defining: standardised generic data structure enough complexity hold observational data metadata, including site-specific, variable-specific individual record-specific metadata; methods/functions converting data formats, combining data different sources, quality control gap-filling.

title: Meta Data for Met Data
text: can install development version metamet GitHub :
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak("NERC-CEH/metamet")

title: Meta Data for Met Data
text: Basic usage first create metamet objects files pre-existing data frames data tables. metadata describing observations available structure, objects can processed relatively easily : rename variables standard naming conventions combine data different sites apply quality control impute missing data. typical workflow go perform tasks adding reference data ECMWF ERA5 reanalysis, join metamet objects, apply quality control algorithms, impute missing values various algorithms, check data manually additional QC. illustrated . Clearly two-dimensional data table sufficient hold information. Instead define metamet data object set related data tables. implement list R, containing six data tables (dt) explained table .
code: library(metamet) fname_dt <- testthat::test_path("data-raw/UK-AMO_BM_dt_2026.csv") fname_meta <- testthat::test_path("data-raw/dt_meta.xlsx") fname_site <- testthat::test_path("data-raw/dt_site.csv")  mm <- metamet(   dt = fname_dt,   dt_meta = fname_meta,   dt_site = fname_site,   site_id = "UK-AMO" ) #> Loading file: tests/testthat/data-raw/UK-AMO_BM_dt_2026.csv #> Reading data table ...  # print the outline strucutre: mm #> metamet object #> ------------- #> Data table (dt) - first 6 cols: #>                    DATECT       TS      SWC          G         WTD WTD_4_1_1 #>                    <POSc>    <num>    <num>      <num>       <num>     <num> #>    1: 2026-01-01 00:30:00 4.704447 66.78342 -5.2438167  0.03166667        NA #>    2: 2026-01-01 01:00:00 4.710880 66.78225 -5.1350167  0.03091667        NA #>    3: 2026-01-01 01:30:00 4.716867 66.78408 -5.0469667  0.02978333        NA #>    4: 2026-01-01 02:00:00 4.722013 66.78121 -4.9933833  0.02945000        NA #>    5: 2026-01-01 02:30:00 4.726623 66.78179 -4.9702167  0.02666667        NA #>   ---                                                                        #> 1484: 2026-01-31 22:00:00 4.702524 53.85774 -0.9039389 -0.18318600  -0.60073 #> 1485: 2026-01-31 22:30:00 4.743937 53.84866 -0.9908333 -0.18335267  -0.60073 #> 1486: 2026-01-31 23:00:00 4.773235 53.84151 -1.0347333 -0.18363933  -0.60073 #> 1487: 2026-01-31 23:30:00 4.783013 53.83243 -1.0497611 -0.18391933  -0.60073 #> 1488: 2026-02-01 00:00:00 4.785532 53.82335 -1.0490556 -0.18402600  -0.60073 #>  #> Metadata (dt_meta) - first 6 cols: #>      site name_dt standard_name      long_name  units        type #>    <char>  <char>        <char>         <char> <char>      <char> #> 1: UK-AMO    site          site           site   <NA>        site #> 2: UK-AMO  D_SNOW        D_SNOW     Snow depth     cm      height #> 3: UK-AMO  DATECT        DATECT      timestamp   <NA>        time #> 4: UK-AMO       G             G Soil heat flux  W m-2 energy flux #> 5: UK-AMO G_4_1_1             G Soil heat flux  W m-2 energy flux #> 6: UK-AMO G_4_1_2             G Soil heat flux  W m-2 energy flux #>  #> Site information (dt_site): #>      site        long_name   lon   lat  elev #>    <char>           <char> <num> <num> <int> #> 1: UK-AMO Auchencorth Moss -3.25 55.75   120 #>  #> QC information (dt_qc) - first 6 cols: #> NULL #>  #> Reference data (dt_ref) - first 6 cols: #> NULL mm <- add_era5(   mm,   fname_era5 = testthat::test_path("data-raw/dt_era5.csv") ) mm <- join(mm, mm_old) mm <- apply_qc(mm) mm <- impute(mm = mm) run_shiny()

what: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
text: Reads ERA5 meteorological data attaches `metamet` object reference data (`dt_ref`). Converts accumulated precipitation rate, renames variables, ensures temporal alignment observed reference data. Optionally restricts date ranges harmonizes time resolution main observations.

title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
code: add_era5(   mm,   fname_era5 = "data-raw/UK-AMO/dt_era5.csv",   restrict_ref_to_obs = TRUE,   restrict_obs_to_ref = FALSE,   report_end_interval = TRUE,   first_date = NULL,   last_date = NULL,   extra_rows = 2 )

title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
text: mm `metamet` object containing observation data metadata. fname_era5 Path CSV file containing ERA5 data. restrict_ref_to_obs Logical. `TRUE` (default), restricts reference data date range observations. restrict_obs_to_ref Logical. `TRUE`, restricts observations date range reference data. report_end_interval Logical. time reported end (`TRUE`, default) start (`FALSE`) -averaging interval. first_date Optional. Earliest date include (overrides automatic date selection set). last_date Optional. Latest date include (overrides automatic date selection set). extra_rows Integer. Extra rows add time-averaging padding.

title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
text: `metamet` object `mm`, processed ERA5 data available `mm$dt_ref`.

title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
text: - Converts ERA5 precipitation ("tp") sum (mm) rate (mm/s). - Renames variables ERA5 data match main observations according `dt_meta`. - Optionally limits ERA5 data match observation period, vice versa. - Ensures reference observed data tables matching time resolutions,   aggregating disaggregating needed. - Warns dimensions become mismatched.

title: Add ERA5 reference data to a `metamet` object — add_era5
code: # mm <- add_era5(mm)

what: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
text: Applies quality control procedures `metamet` object removing --range values creating quality code table. function flags missing invalid values fall outside acceptable ranges defined metadata.

title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
code: apply_qc(mm0)

title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
text: mm0 `metamet` object containing least `dt` (data table), `dt_meta` (metadata table min/max ranges), `dt_site` (site information).

title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
text: modified `metamet` object : dt Data values, --range values set NA dt_qc New quality control table (1 = invalid/missing, 0 = valid) fields input object preserved.

title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
text: function performs following steps: Removes values outside min/max range specified `dt_meta` Creates quality control table (`dt_qc`) 1 indicates missing         --range value, 0 indicates valid value Adds validator field marked "auto" (replaced username         manually validated)

title: Apply Quality Control Checks to Meteorological Data — apply_qc
code: # mm_qc <- apply_qc(mm)

what: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
text: Imputes missing flagged values one variables metamet object using various methods. function supports regression-based imputation, time-series smoothing (GAM), substitution reference data (ERA5), physical constraints. imputed values flagged quality control (QC) table.

title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
code: impute(   v_y = NULL,   mm,   method = NULL,   qc_tokeep = 0,   selection = TRUE,   k = 40,   fit = TRUE,   n_min = 10,   x = NULL,   lat = 55.792,   lon = -3.243,   plot_graph = TRUE )

title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
text: v_y Character vector variable names (quoted strings) impute. NULL (default), variables data table except site time selected imputation. mm metamet object containing observation data (dt), quality control codes (dt_qc), optional reference data (dt_ref). method Character string specifying imputation method use. NULL (default), method read imputation_method column dt_meta. Supported methods: "time" Generalized additive model (GAM) smoothing splines     time hour day. Suitable variables strong diurnal/seasonal patterns. "regn" Linear regression covariate x. Fits model     excluding missing values, predicts. "era5" Substitute ERA5 reanalysis data dt_ref.     fewer n_min observations, replaces directly without fitting. "noneg" Replace negative values zero (physical constraint). "nightzero" Replace nighttime values zero. Uses site coordinates     (lat, lon) identify day/night via openair::cutData(). "zero" Replace missing/flagged values zero. qc_tokeep Integer QC code(s) indicating "good" "raw" data retain unchanged. Default 0. Data QC codes qc_tokeep candidates imputation. selection Logical. TRUE (default), applies selection filtering metadata. FALSE, imputes values matching qc_tokeep criteria. k Integer. Smoothing basis dimension GAM "time" method (default: 40). Automatically reduced data sparse. Controls temporal smoothness. fit Logical. TRUE (default), fits regression/GAM models imputation. FALSE, uses direct substitution (useful "era5" method minimal data). n_min Integer. Minimum number non-missing observations required fit model (default: 10). fewer observations exist, "time" "regn" methods skip imputation; "era5" method switches direct substitution. x Optional. Character string naming covariate column data table use "regn" method. example, x = "PPFD_IN" regress photosynthetic photon flux density. lat Numeric. Latitude site degrees (default: 55.792). Used "nightzero" method calculate sunrise/sunset times. lon Numeric. Longitude site degrees (default: -3.243). Used "nightzero" method calculate sunrise/sunset times. plot_graph Logical. TRUE (default), generates diagnostic plots showing observations, reference data (available), QC flags. Saves PNG files output/ directory naming convention plot_<variable>_<method>.png.

title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
text: input metamet object mm, invisibly returned updated   dt (imputed values) dt_qc (new QC codes imputed points).

title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
text: **Imputation Process:** function iterates variable v_y. variable: 1. Determines imputation method (parameter metadata). 2. Identifies rows impute based QC codes selection flag. 3. Applies selected imputation method. 4. Updates QC table flag imputed values. 5. Optionally generates diagnostic plot. **Minimum Data Handling:** fewer n_min non-missing observations exist: - "time" "regn" methods skip variable (imputation). - "era5" method switches direct substitution (fit = FALSE). - methods ("zero", "noneg", "nightzero") unaffected. **Data Reference:** function requires metadata table (dt_meta) describing variables, optionally reference table (dt_ref) ERA5 reanalysis data. Ensure present metamet object. **Plotting:** Diagnostic plots overlay observations (colored QC code), reference data (black line), imputed points. Useful validating imputation results identifying issues.

title: Impute missing values in meteorological data — impute
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example 1: Impute from metadata method specification mm <- impute(   v_y = "SW_IN",   mm = mm,   qc_tokeep = 0,   plot_graph = TRUE )  # Example 2: Impute using ERA5 data, multiple variables mm <- impute(   v_y = c("TA", "RH"),   mm = mm,   method = "era5",   fit = FALSE,   plot_graph = TRUE )  # Example 3: Regression imputation with covariate mm <- impute(   v_y = "SW_IN",   mm = mm,   method = "regn",   x = "PPFD_IN",   fit = TRUE,   n_min = 15 ) } # }

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
text: Combine two `metamet` objects performing full join `site` time variable. time variable names differ two objects, `mm2`'s time column corresponding metadata row renamed match `mm1` prior joining.

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
code: join(mm1, mm2)

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
text: mm1 `metamet` object (list containing least `dt`, `dt_meta`, `dt_site`). mm2 `metamet` object joined onto `mm1`. Values `mm2` take precedence conflicts occur.

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
text: `metamet` object merged `dt`, `dt_meta`, `dt_site` (  returned object structure `mm2` contains combined   information inputs).

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
text: join performed using `powerjoin::power_full_join()` main data table (`dt`), metadata table (`dt_meta`) site table (`dt_site`). Conflict resolution uses `coalesce_yx`, prefers values second object (`mm2`) objects contain differing values field.

title: Join two `metamet` objects — join
code: # mm_joined <- join(mm1, mm2)

what: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
title: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
text: Constructor function creating `metamet` objects files data tables. generic function methods different input types, allowing flexible object creation CSV/Excel files, data frames, data tables.

title: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
code: metamet(dt, ...)  # Default S3 method metamet(dt, ...)  # S3 method for class 'character' metamet(   dt,   dt_meta = NULL,   dt_site = NULL,   dt_qc = NULL,   dt_ref = NULL,   site_id = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' metamet(   dt,   dt_meta = NULL,   dt_site = NULL,   dt_qc = NULL,   dt_ref = NULL,   site_id = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'data.table' metamet(   dt,   dt_meta = NULL,   dt_site = NULL,   dt_qc = NULL,   dt_ref = NULL,   site_id = NULL,   ... )

title: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
text: dt main data input. `metamet.character`, file path CSV format readable `data.table::fread()`. `metamet.data.frame` `metamet.data.table`, actual data object. ... Additional arguments passed methods. dt_meta `data.table`, `data.frame`, file path metadata describing columns `dt`. Excel files (.xlsx) supported; otherwise CSV format assumed. Required. dt_site `data.table`, `data.frame`, file path site metadata containing site-level information. Required. dt_qc Optional quality control data; can `data.table`, `data.frame`, file path. Defaults `NULL`. dt_ref Optional reference data (e.g., ERA5 reanalysis); can `data.table`, `data.frame`, file path. Defaults `NULL`. site_id character string specifying site identifier. Required `metamet.character` method. Used tag observations.

title: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
text: `metamet` object containing provided data metadata,   processed validated structure.

title: Create a `metamet` object from various input types — metamet
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create from files mm <- metamet(   dt = "data.csv",   dt_meta = "metadata.xlsx",   dt_site = "site_info.csv",   site_id = "UK-AMO" )  # Create from data tables (requires metadata and site data) mm <- metamet(   dt = my_data_table,   dt_meta = my_metadata_table,   dt_site = my_site_info_table,   site_id = "UK-AMO" ) } # }

title: Example `metamet` objects — mm1
text: Three example `metamet` objects containing meteorological data Auchencorth Moss (UK-AMO) site.

title: Example `metamet` objects — mm1
text: object `metamet` list containing: `dt` data table observations columns time, site,       various meteorological variables (temperature, wind speed, wind       direction, precipitation, etc.). `dt_meta` metadata table describing column `dt`,       including variable type, units, attributes. `dt_site` table containing site-level information       location (latitude/longitude) elevation.

text: Auchencorth Moss (UK-AMO) site data

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
text: Aggregates meteorological data `metamet` object specified time intervals. function handles different variable types appropriately: precipitation summed, variables (temperature, wind speed, etc.) averaged. Wind direction vector-averaged present.

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
code: time_average(   mm_in,   avg.time = "30 min",   report_end_interval = TRUE,   extra_rows = 0 )

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
text: mm_in `metamet` object containing least `dt`, `dt_meta`, `dt_site`. avg.time Time interval averaging; passed `openair::timeAverage()`. Default `"30 min"`. report_end_interval Logical. `TRUE` (default), returned timestamps represent end averaging interval. `FALSE`, timestamps represent start interval. extra_rows Integer number time intervals add data; usually truncated averaging.

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
text: `metamet` object time-averaged `dt`, `dt_qc`, `dt_ref`   tables (applicable). object structure preserved.

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
text: function uses `openair::timeAverage()` aggregation preserves structure input object, including quality control (`dt_qc`) reference (`dt_ref`) tables present.

title: Time-average a `metamet` object — time_average
code: # mm_avg <- time_average(mm, avg.time = "1 hour")

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
text: Aggregates meteorological data data table specified time intervals. Handles flexible variable naming, wind direction vector averaging, configurable reporting interval start end times. Uses openair::timeAverage() aggregation support custom statistics (mean, median, sum, etc.).

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
code: time_average_dt(   dt_in,   avg.time = "30 min",   statistic = "mean",   first_date = NULL,   last_date = NULL,   time_name = NULL,   wd_name = NULL,   ws_name = NULL,   report_end_interval = TRUE,   extra_rows = 2 )

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
text: dt_in data table containing meteorological observations time column. avg.time Time interval averaging (e.g., "30 min", "1 hour"). Default: "30 min". Passed openair::timeAverage(). statistic Character string specifying aggregation statistic ("mean", "median", "sum", etc.). Default: "mean". Use "median" quality control codes. first_date Optional POSIXct date. Earliest date include. NULL, uses minimum date data. last_date Optional POSIXct date. Latest date include. NULL, uses maximum date data. time_name Character string. Name time column dt_in (e.g., "time"). wd_name Character string. Name wind direction column, present. Used vector averaging. ws_name Character string. Name wind speed column, present. Used vector averaging. report_end_interval Logical. TRUE (default), timestamps report end averaging interval. FALSE, timestamps report start interval (default openair behavior). extra_rows Integer (default: 2). Number extra time intervals include specified date range, used padding averaging.

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
text: data table time-averaged values, preserving original time column name   variable structure. "site" column stored character.

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
text: - Creates temporary copy input data avoid modifying original reference. - Renames time column "date" compatibility openair::timeAverage(). - Optionally renames wind direction wind speed columns proper vector averaging. - Pads averaging window extra intervals (controlled extra_rows) restricts   results original date range. - Converts factor columns (e.g., "site") character consistency. - Uses "nocb" (next observation carried backward) fill leading missing values. - Restores original time column names output.

title: Time-average a data table — time_average_dt
code: # dt_averaged <- time_average_dt( #   dt_in = my_dt, #   avg.time = "1 hour", #   time_name = "time", #   wd_name = "wind_direction", #   ws_name = "wind_speed" # )

what: Roxygen2 Documentation Workflow Guide for metamet Package
title: Roxygen2 Documentation Workflow Guide for metamet Package
text: guide explains add, manage, generate roxygen2 documentation metamet package.

title: Roxygen2 Documentation Workflow Guide for metamet Package
text: Roxygen2 R documentation system lets write documentation comments directly R code automatically generates .Rd files updates NAMESPACE file.

title: Roxygen2 Documentation Workflow Guide for metamet Package
text: Documentation comments start ##' (space #) Tags like @param, @return, @export control documentation generation devtools::document() roxygen2::roxygenise() regenerates documentation files

what: 2. Structure of a Roxygen Documentation Block
title: Roxygen2 Documentation Workflow Guide for metamet Package
text: ```r ##’ Brief description (one line) ##’ ##’ Detailed description (optional, multiple lines allowed). ##’ Explain function works. ##’ ##’ @param param_name Description parameter. ##’ @param another_param Description another parameter. ##’ ##’ @return Description function returns. ##’ ##’ @details ##’ Additional implementation details, notes, considerations. ##’ ##’ @seealso ##’  related functionality ##’ ##’ @examples ##’ # Example 1: Basic usage ##’ result <- my_function(x = 1) ##’ ##’ # Example 2: advanced usage ##’ result <- my_function(x = c(1, 2, 3)) ##’ ##’ @export ##’ @keywords internal my_function <- function(param1, param2 = NULL) { # Function body }

title: metamet (development version)
text: Initial CRAN submission.
